mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below -0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median 60-day returns, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production reflects supply-side bottlenecks that constrain output capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when applied outside late-cycle regimes, or 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5 percent, monetary policy tightens financial conditions sufficiently to suppress equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered windows, or the threshold condition fails to separate return distributions (KS test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative median returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into positive equity risk premiums, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias (median near zero) or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production alongside rising personal consumption expenditures indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery that boosts corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to generate positive alpha (Sharpe < 0.3) during post-shock periods, or returns are negative in > 55% of synchronized expansion windows.
